title: Filtering the decision list
question: How should you narrow down which decisions are shown — beyond just searching?
rationale: Put all controls in one calm bar — search as the hero, plus Sort / Filter / View on the right. The Filter button opens a drawer (open-only, categories, second dimension) with a count badge. Chosen over always-visible chips, which cluttered as categories grew, and search-only, which couldn't focus one category. Sort is now a dropdown menu (not a pill row), so the grouping choices stay collapsed as they multiply.

history.from: Controls split across the bar — the By phase / Recent switch on the far left, a width-capped search in the middle, Filter and a lone view icon on the right
history.reason: Reorganized into search-as-hero (full-width, on the left) plus one labeled Sort · Filter · View pill cluster on the right, so all the controls read as a single coherent bar

history.from: Filter button opened a small pop-up anchored under it (open-only, categories, second dimension) with a count badge
history.reason: Moved the filter controls into a right-side drawer that reuses the decision sheet's overlay — backdrop, blur, and Esc-to-close — so the two overlays feel like one system and the filter list has room to scroll as categories grow

history.from: Clear filters was a faint text link at the end of the scrolling filter list, easy to miss below the fold
history.reason: Pinned Clear filters to a bordered button in a footer fixed at the bottom of the drawer (showing the active count, e.g. "Clear filters (3)"), so it stays visible no matter how far the list scrolls

history.from: Sort was a segmented pill cluster — Recent · By phase, with By <axis> and By version appended as more pills — sitting beside Filter and View
history.reason: Collapsed Sort into a single dropdown button that opens a popover menu, so the grouping choices stay tidy as they grow (up to four: Recent, By phase, By <axis>, By version) instead of widening the bar; the button now shows the active mode as its label

options.label: Filter button + drawer (open, category, area), search made prominent

tradeoffs.note: focus on a category or just the open decisions, alongside search
the drawer scrolls, holding many categories without cluttering the bar
one tidy bar; filters tucked behind a button with a count badge
the drawer reuses the decision sheet's backdrop, blur, and Esc-to-close
a slide-in drawer, multi-select state, and a consolidated control bar
a growing row of chips crowds the bar as categories pile up
can't isolate a category or the open decisions without typing
